data.title: Irish authorities access lost Bitcoin wallet holding 500 BTC linked to drug dealer, moved to Coinbase.
data.description: On Tuesday, it was reported that a crypto wallet linked to a convicted drug dealer, who is believed to have misplaced his wallet keys, sent 500 Bitcoin to Coinbase.

data.title: Investors are genuinely accumulating Bitcoin as seen in exchange outflows, impacting its trading within a narrow range.
data.description: According to crypto analysts, the current stability in Bitcoin trading could be attributed to the continuous accumulation of the cryptocurrency. This accumulation is seen as one of the key reasons why Bitcoin is trading within a narrow range.

data.title: Increasing US Treasury yields, Iran conflict, and inflation fears suppress Bitcoin price; tech stocks plummet as bond yields rise, stifling Bitcoin's growth.
data.description: The decline in technology stock prices and the increase in bond yields have led to a surge in demand for cash, hindering Bitcoin from experiencing any significant bullish movement.

data.title: CFTC head starts innovation group aiming to create crypto rules, working with industry on AI, prediction markets.
data.description: A team of experts will collaborate with members of the industry to create strategies for regulating cryptocurrency, artificial intelligence, and prediction markets. The main focus will be on exploring innovative methods to address these emerging technologies and ensure compliance. The task force aims to leverage diverse perspectives to develop effective regulatory frameworks that balance innovation with risk management. These initiatives will involve input from various stakeholders and aim to stay at the forefront of advancements in these fields.

data.title: BitGo and Susquehanna introduce OTC prediction market trading for institutions to trade event contracts using crypto collateral amidst US regulatory focus.
data.description: Institutional investors now have the opportunity to engage in trading event-based contracts with the use of crypto collateral. This move comes at a time when the regulatory scrutiny surrounding prediction markets is escalating in the United States. The offering enables investors to leverage cryptocurrency as collateral for trading these contracts, amidst growing attention on prediction market activities.

data.title: BTC price drops to $69.5K on geopolitical concerns, hinting at a market shift as analysts observe changing behavior.
data.description: The price of Bitcoin dropped below $70,000 against the backdrop of geopolitical tensions. Analysts are observing indications that suggest a possible change in market dynamics.

data.title: BrightGene Announces Positive Topline Phase 1 Results of Oral BGM0504 in Obesity in China and the United States
data.description: BrightGene Bio-Medical Technology Co., Ltd. reported that BGM0504 has demonstrated good tolerability. The overall safety, pharmacokinetic (pk), and efficacy profile indicate that once-daily oral dosing is suitable. The announcement was made in Suzhou, China on March 25, 2026. BrightGene, listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange as 688166, revealed positive topline results related to BGM0504.
Australia Home Affairs Minister: Iran conflict raises risk some temporary visa holders may not depart on expiry.
Australia to temporarily restrict entry for Iranian visitor visa holders abroad, Home Affairs Minister says.

data.title: How China maps out a strong start for 15th Five-Year Plan
data.description: On March 25, 2026 in Beijing, it was reported that China's five-year plans are crucial medium- and long-term development strategies created in cycles of five years. These plans have been instrumental in China's governance success, with the country following them since the 1950s. The plans serve as blueprints for China's development and progress, highlighting key strategies and objectives for growth.

data.title: Strauss Group Reports Q4 & FY-2025 Financial Results:¹ FY-2025 Revenues of NIS 12.5 billion, up c.12% y-o-y, with strong EBIT exceeding NIS 1 billion, up c.36% y-o-y
data.description: The rise in profitability was primarily attributed to the success of Coffee International, particularly with record-breaking earnings from 3corações (a 50% joint venture in Brazil). In terms of key financial highlights for the full year of 2025, Strauss Group witnessed revenues of NIS 12,507 million, marking an 11.6% increase. This growth rate excludes the impact of currency fluctuations and any activities that were divested during this period.

data.title: SafePaaS Announces Federated Identity Governance Architecture for Multi-Cloud and AI NHI
data.description: On March 25, 2026, in San Francisco, SafePaaS introduced a new identity governance system at RSAC™ 2026. This architecture is tailored for businesses working in multiple cloud settings and handling the increasing number of AI-powered non-human identities (NHIs). This solution is essential for organizations grappling with the growth of NHIs and operating in various cloud environments.
Strauss Group Reports Q4 & FY-2025 Financial Results:¹ FY-2025 Revenues of NIS 12.5 billion, up c.12% y-o-y, with strong EBIT exceeding NIS 1 billion, up c.36% y-o-y

data.title: CROSSJECT publishes its 2025 financial results and confirms the strengthening of its operational, industrial and financial trajectory
data.description: In Dijon, France, on 25th March 2026 at 07:20 AM CET, CROSSJECT, a pharmaceutical company specializing in emergency products utilizing the ZENEO® needle-free auto-injector technology, is progressing with the development and registration of ZEPIZURE®, an injectable for treating epileptic seizures. The company, listed on Euronext under the ticker ALCJ and ISIN FR0011716265, has released its financial report for the fiscal year ending on 31st December 2025.

data.title: Trump's interest in Iran talks leads to sharp drop in oil prices amid global uncertainty.
data.description: According to recent data, there has been a significant increase in job openings in the United States. The number of job openings reached a record high of over 10 million, indicating a strong demand for labor across various industries. This surge in job openings comes as companies ramp up hiring efforts amidst the ongoing economic recovery. Additionally, the number of people voluntarily quitting their jobs also increased to nearly 4 million, suggesting that workers are feeling more confident in their ability to find better opportunities. Economists see these trends as positive signs for the job market and overall economic growth.
Abu Dhabi index up 0.7% in early trading, LSEG reports.
A Russian attack has damaged an energy facility in Ukraine’s Chernihiv region, cutting power to nearly 150,000 consumers, the company said.
ASX 200 advances 1.9% to 8,534.30 points at the close, reflecting broad-based gains across the market.
Russian attack kills one in Ukraine’s Odesa region, emergency services say.

data.title: Bitcoin Doesn't Face 'Existential' Risk From Quantum Computers, Says Brian Armstrong: 'With Good Work, We Can Make Sure That Lands In A Good Place'
data.description: The CEO of Coinbase Global Inc. (NASDAQ: COIN), Brian Armstrong, downplayed quantum computing threats to Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) and cryptocurrencies. In a recent interview with Norges Bank Investment Management CEO Nicolai Tangen, Armstrong emphasized the need for "diligent effort" to address quantum risks, stating that they are not an "existential" threat to Bitcoin. Armstrong expressed confidence that through hard work, the potential risks can be mitigated effectively. Despite concerns about a bear market, Armstrong believes that the fundamentals of BTC remain strong. He noted his surprise at the significant drop in Bitcoin's value but maintained that the core principles supporting the cryptocurrency have not shifted significantly. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
